steps.title: Identify the structure at the blank
steps.content: The left side contains an independent clause ending with the noun “mouthpiece.” The right side begins another independent clause: “A double reed contains...” The blank must provide a relative clause that modifies “mouthpiece.”

steps.title: Apply the relative clause word order rule
steps.content:
Relative clauses introduced by “where” require declarative word order: “where sound is made” (subject + verb).

Choices B and D use interrogative inversion, which violates this rule; choice C uses the correct word order but ends with a question mark instead of a period.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
